title: Fracttalix Sentinel: Three-Channel Streaming Anomaly Detector
description: Fracttalix: a research corpus comprising the Fractal Rhythm Model (FRM), Meta-Kaizen series, and the Dual Reader Standard (DRS). The FRM describes association dynamics via f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ) across scales from quantum decoherence to cosmological structure. The Meta-Kaizen series (6 papers) derives a measurement-theoretic governance framework from six axioms. The DRS is a verification architecture comprising two protocols: the Dual Reader Protocol (DRP) for text and the Grounded Verification Protocol (GVP) for software. Every claim carries a machine-evaluable falsification predicate (Kernel K = P, O, M, B) that reduces to embedded binary logic — a deterministic single-bit verdict independent of human language. Includes Fracttalix Sentinel, a three-channel streaming anomaly detector implementing the FRM (zero dependencies). 22 corpus objects, 121 machine-readable claims with falsification predicates, 18 AI layers, and full DRS compliance.
notes: Theoretical foundation: Fractal Rhythm Model (FRM) — 11 axioms. Meta-Kaizen series — 6 papers deriving KVS from measurement-theoretic axioms and extending the DRS to software. Dual Reader Standard (DRS) — verification architecture with DRP (text protocol) and GVP (software protocol). Falsification Kernel K=(P,O,M,B) reduces every claim to embedded binary logic. 22 corpus objects across two tracks. 121 claims, 18 AI layers, schema v3-S51. Introduced by Thomas Brennan with AI collaborators Claude (Anthropic) and Grok (xAI).
